location.office_time_required: occasionally required to work out of assigned Affirm office for proximal roles

level_signals: 8 years of proven experience managing technical programs
Actively mentors and levels up team members
Base pay range $195K-$255K (CA/WA/NY/NJ/CT) consistent with Staff IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
8 years of proven experience managing technical programs.
Passion and drive to change consumer finance for the better.
Organized and strategic approach to collaborating across multi-disciplinary teams.
Obsesses over getting the details right but knows how to ship on time.
Working and driving strategic programs and building a remote friendly culture.
Actively mentors and levels up team members.
Track record of successfully driving complex large technology applications and geographically distributed backend systems.
Track record of strong stakeholder management with Engineering, Product, Design, Analytics, and other cross-functional teams.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Prior experience in FinTech, Payments or Commerce is a plus.

scope.surface: Consumer product suite and platform capabilities across Engineering and Product teams
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B buy-now-pay-later consumer credit
core_responsibilities: Drive complex, multi-disciplinary, and highly visible technical programs in service of expanding consumer product suite and platform capabilities

Coordinate across engineering teams and non-engineering stakeholders through the full program lifecycle to plan requirements, identify risks, manage program schedules, and communicate clearly with cross-functional partners

Understand technical implementation at the architecture level and propose technical alternatives, ask questions that clarify priorities and push the team to be effective, and develop domain and technical knowledge
Partner with Product and Engineering to plan requirements, identify risks, manage program schedules, and communicate clearly with cross-functional partners across the company
Track progress, resolve dependencies, evaluate risks, and communicate status to upper management and program stakeholders, and engage the program teams to achieve goals

compensation.equity_form: Equity Grade - 10; ESPP (employee stock purchase plan enabling you to buy shares of Affirm at a discount); equity rewards mentioned as part of total compensation

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and competitive ($195K-$255K base for high-cost states)
Remote-first company with majority of roles remote
100% subsidized medical coverage for employee and dependents
Transparent pay structure explained in JD
ESPP and equity rewards included in total compensation
Flexible spending wallets for tech, food, lifestyle, and family forming expenses
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for non-citizens
Proximal role holders may occasionally be required to work from an Affirm office — role location flexibility could vary
Pay Grade - N and Equity Grade - 10 labels are internal identifiers with no external benchmark provided

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title, 8+ years requirement, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd uses CA/WA/NY/NJ/CT tier as primary range since it is the higher and more commonly referenced tier
ambiguities: The JD is tagged #LI-Remote and describes Affirm as remote-first, but also notes proximal roles may occasionally require office attendance. It is not specified whether this Staff TPM role is remote or proximal — treated as remote-us based on #LI-Remote tag and remote-first framing.
Two pay ranges are stated: $195K-$255K (CA, WA, NY, NJ, CT) and $173K-$233K (all other U.S. states). The higher range is used as the primary value in compensation.base_range_usd.
